citeinfo.title: United States Five-Digit ZIP Code Areas, 2022

descript.abstract: U.S. ZIP Code Areas represents five-digit ZIP Code areas used by the U.S. Postal Service to deliver mail more effectively. The first digit of a five-digit ZIP Code divides the United States into 10 large groups of states (or equivalent areas) numbered from 0 in the Northeast to 9 in the far West. Within these areas, each state is divided into an average of 10 smaller geographical areas, identified by the second and third digits. These digits, in conjunction with the first digit, represent a Sectional Center Facility (SCF) or a mail processing facility area. The fourth and fifth digits identify a post office, station, branch or local delivery area.
descript.purpose: U.S. ZIP Code Areas provides ZIP Code, postal district name, population, and area for the ZIP Code areas in the United States.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States- Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing and consulting services to your local Esri Regional Office. For support, contact Esri by telephone (voice) between 5:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. Pacific time, Monday through Friday, by dialing +1-888-377-4575; facsimile (fax) available at +1-909-792-0960; or visit https://support.esri.com/; Esri holidays excluded. Email and chat are available through the Support page on My Esri (https://my.esri.com).
Outside the United States- Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing, sales, support, and consulting services to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0. For other questions or comments, you may contact Esri headquarters by email, telephone, or fax or write to us.

dataqual.logic: The dataset is converted to file geodatabase (FGDB) format using tools in ArcGIS. The geometry is checked, and if needed repaired.
dataqual.complete: After processing, the dataset is checked for drawing display and number of records and file sizes compared with source materials.

horizpa.horizpar: Horizontal positional accuracy is dependent on geographic area. In most areas, accuracy is between 5 and 25 meters (16 and 82 feet). In some areas, accuracy is off by up to several tens of meters (100 feet or more). In the poorest areas, accuracy is off by up to 100 meters (328 feet).

citeinfo.title: Postal Districts, Postal District Names; Administrative Area Order 1

serinfo.sername: TomTom MultiNet Post NAM; TomTom MultiNet North America

srcinfo.srccitea: TomTom MultiNet Post North America; TomTom MultiNet North America

procstep.procdesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Extracted the dataset from TomTom MultiNet Post North America. Dissolved the multi-part features. Joined the Postal District Names table to the Postal Districts feature class to get the field PO_NAME. Added the field STATE and coded it by overlaying on Administrative Area Order 1 dataset from TomTom MultiNet North America. Added the field POPULATION (alias POP2021). Added the field SQMI and calculated its values. Added and calculated the field POP_SQMI (alias POP21_SQMI). Removed unneeded fields.

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

enttyp.enttypd: The polygons represent five-digit ZIP Code areas where mail is delivered (from a city block or two to a whole rural town) in the United States.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The five-digit number used by the postal service to identify an area where mail is delivered.

codesetd.codesetn: USPS National 5-Digit ZIP Code™ and Post Office Directory
USPS National 5-Digit ZIP Code and Post Office Directory

attr.attrdef: The two-letter abbreviation for the state or territory in which the ZIP Code area is located.

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 38-2009, (Formerly FIPS 5-2) Codes for the Identification of the States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attr.attrdef: The 2021 population of the ZIP Code area.

attrdomv.udom: Populations for the features. A <Null> value means no data.

attr.attrdef: The 2021 population of the ZIP Code area per square mile.

attrdomv.udom: Calculated populations per square mile for the features. A <Null> value means no data.

attr.attrdef: The area of the ZIP Code polygon in square miles using the North America Albers Equal Area Conic projection.
Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States, contact the Esri Sales staff at +1-800-447-9778 or visit https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=1 for more information about our software and data.
Outside the United States, direct all inquiries to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0.

distinfo.distliab: See access and use constraints information.

digtinfo.formcont: To use this data requires software that works with FGDB files.

stdorder.ordering: Data and Maps is available only as part of ArcGIS software.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
